name: Vladimir Putin
role: President of Russia (2000–2008, 2012–present)
quote: Russia is not just a country — it is a civilization. You either respect Russia or you try to destroy it. There is no middle ground.

name: Dmitry Medvedev
role: Deputy Chairman of Russia's Security Council; former President (2008–2012)
quote: The loss of the war by a nuclear power could mean the start of a nuclear war. Nuclear powers have never been defeated in major conflicts vital to their security.

name: Sergei Lavrov
role: Minister of Foreign Affairs of Russia (2004–present)
quote: The West declared total war on Russia. We didn't start this war. The war was started by those in Washington who sponsored the coup in Kyiv in 2014.

name: Valery Gerasimov
role: Chief of the General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces; Supreme Commander of Forces in Ukraine (2023–present)
quote: The role of non-military means of achieving political and strategic goals has grown and in many cases has exceeded the power of force of weapons in their effectiveness.

name: Andrei Belousov
role: Minister of Defense of Russia (2024–present); economist and former First Deputy PM
quote: The defense industry must be integrated into the broader economic strategy. We need sustainable military production that the economy can support for the long term.

name: Nikolai Patrushev
role: Former FSB Director (1999–2008); former Secretary of the Security Council (2008–2023); now Putin's aide
quote: The United States has set itself the task of destroying Russia as a state. Not weakening it, not pushing it back — but eliminating it as a geopolitical subject.

name: Alexei Navalny
role: Opposition leader, anti-corruption activist; died in prison February 16, 2024
quote: Russia will be free. I am absolutely certain of this. You don't need to be afraid. Come out and protest. They can't jail all of us.

name: Yulia Navalnaya
role: Opposition leader; widow of Navalny; continues his political work from exile
quote: Putin killed my husband. He is a murderer. And today I am turning to you, Putin. You killed half of me, my family. But I will still continue the cause of Navalny.

name: Mikhail Khodorkovsky
role: Former oligarch (Yukos); imprisoned 2003–2013; now opposition figure in exile
quote: Putin is afraid of his own people. Every honest election, every independent court, every free newspaper is a threat to his power. That is why he destroys them all.

name: Vladimir Kara-Murza
role: Russian opposition politician; US citizen; survived two poisonings; imprisoned 2022–2024; exchanged in August 2024 swap
quote: This regime will end. There will be a Russia after Putin — a democratic, peaceful Russia that will be a partner to the rest of the world.

name: Volodymyr Zelensky
role: President of Ukraine (2019–present); wartime leader
quote: We are all here. I am here. We are not laying down our arms. We will be defending our country. Glory to our defenders! Glory to Ukraine!

name: Alexander Lukashenko
role: President of Belarus (1994–present); key Russian ally; allowed Russian forces to stage through Belarus for Kyiv attack
quote: If Ukraine attacks Belarus, we will enter the war alongside Russia. There is no other option for us — we are bound by the Union State.

name: Xi Jinping
role: General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party; President of China; key Putin partner
quote: China and Russia have maintained the strategic resolve of a 'no limits' partnership. We have become each other's most trustworthy strategic partners.

name: Donald Trump
role: US President (2025–present); pursuing Ukraine-Russia ceasefire negotiations
quote: I will have the war in Ukraine ended within 24 hours — possibly before I take office. Both sides are tired of fighting. A deal can be made.

name: Ursula von der Leyen
role: President of the European Commission (2019–present); led EU sanctions coordination on Russia
quote: We will not allow Putin to divide our Union. European unity is the strongest weapon we have — stronger than any missile. Russia's aggression will fail.

name: Jens Stoltenberg
role: NATO Secretary General (2014–2024); oversaw NATO's largest military buildup since Cold War
quote: NATO is a defensive alliance. But we will not allow Putin's aggression to succeed. Ukraine will become a member of NATO — that is our commitment.

name: Yevgeny Prigozhin
role: Wagner Group founder and commander; led June 2023 mutiny; died in plane crash August 23, 2023
quote: We are dying. Not from Ukrainian bullets, but from the Russian Ministry of Defense's incompetence. The war was launched on false pretexts. The 'denazification' objective is a lie.

name: Mikhail Gorbachev
role: Last leader of the USSR (1985–1991); Nobel Peace Prize 1990; died September 1, 2022
quote: I find it unacceptable that I hear all these reports from Ukraine that are deeply worrying. The use of force against ordinary people is criminal.

name: Viktor Orbán
role: Prime Minister of Hungary; NATO member most aligned with Moscow; blocks EU aid packages
quote: Hungary will not send weapons to Ukraine. We will not join this war. We want peace, and peace cannot be achieved by escalation but by negotiation.

name: Recep Tayyip Erdoğan
role: President of Turkey; key mediator between Russia and Ukraine; sold drones to Ukraine, maintained Russia relations
quote: Turkey is the only country that can talk to both sides. We will not abandon our relations with Russia or Ukraine. Peace requires balance, not taking sides.

name: Vladimir Lenin
role: Founder of the Soviet state; Chairman of the Council of People's Commissars (1917–1924); led Bolshevik Revolution
quote: Without revolutionary theory there can be no revolutionary movement. It is not enough to call ourselves a 'vanguard' — we must act as a vanguard in deeds.

name: Joseph Stalin
role: General Secretary of the Soviet Communist Party (1922–1952); Premier (1941–1953); led the USSR through WWII and the Great Terror
quote: Education is a weapon whose effect depends on who holds it in his hands and at whom it is aimed.

name: Nikita Khrushchev
role: First Secretary of the Soviet Communist Party (1953–1964); initiated de-Stalinization; navigated Cuban Missile Crisis
quote: Stalin acted not through persuasion, explanation, and patient cooperation with people, but by imposing his concepts and demanding absolute submission to his opinion. Whoever opposed this was condemned to moral and physical annihilation.

name: Boris Yeltsin
role: First President of the Russian Federation (1991–1999); dissolved USSR; oversaw chaotic 1990s reforms, shock therapy, and two Chechen wars
quote: I want to ask your forgiveness for failing to justify the hopes of those who believed that we could jump from the grey, stagnant, totalitarian past into a bright, rich, civilised future in one leap.

name: Peter the Great (Peter I)
role: Tsar and first Emperor of Russia (1682–1725); modernized Russia along Western lines; founded St. Petersburg; defeated Sweden in Great Northern War
quote: I have not spared and do not spare my life for my fatherland and people. Russia needs only good examples set by deeds rather than words.

name: Ivan the Terrible (Ivan IV)
role: First Tsar of All Russia (1547–1584); conquered Kazan and Astrakhan Khanates; began Siberian expansion; created the oprichnina secret police
quote: We are free to reward our servants and we are free to execute them. I rule my people by God's will, not by human judgment.

name: Maria Zakharova
role: Director of the Information and Press Department, Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (2015–present); chief MFA spokeswoman
quote: The West wages a systematic information war against Russia. They don't want the world to hear our narrative — so they ban our media and call it freedom of press. We will respond with truth.

name: Aleksandr Dugin
role: Neo-Eurasianist philosopher and ideologue; author of 'Foundations of Geopolitics'; daughter Darya Dugina killed in 2022 car bomb attack near Moscow
quote: Russia must be an empire or it will not exist at all. The Russian people are not simply a nation but a civilization — the carrier of a special historical mission to organize Eurasia.

name: Ilya Yashin
role: Russian opposition municipal politician; imprisoned December 2022 for 8.5 years for 'spreading false information' about Bucha massacres; released in August 2024 prisoner swap
quote: I was jailed for talking about Bucha — for saying that Russian soldiers killed civilians. This is not false information; this is a documented reality confirmed by satellite imagery, journalists, and international investigators.

name: Mikhail Mishustin
role: Prime Minister of Russia (2020–present); technocrat economist who oversaw economic management under sweeping Western sanctions
quote: Russia's economy has demonstrated remarkable resilience in the face of unprecedented external pressure. We have redirected trade flows, developed domestic production, and ensured the stability of the financial system.
